content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Restricted Codex fast-mode suffix handling to `BackendFamily::Codex` so trailing `-fast` still strips to the base Codex model and adds `-c service_tier="fast"`, while OpenRouter requests now pass `...-fast` model IDs through unchanged. Updated the shared model parser in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` to take an explicit fast-mode toggle and added OpenRouter new-session and resume regression coverage in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs` alongside the existing Codex fast-mode assertions.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the Codex/OpenRouter process backend command builders and confirm the reported regression path where OpenRouter reused the new Codex fast-suffix parsing.
2. [Completed] Update `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` so `-fast` parsing and `service_tier="fast"` injection are enabled only for Codex invocations while preserving existing reasoning-effort suffix parsing.
3. [Completed] Add regression tests proving OpenRouter model IDs ending in `-fast` remain unchanged for both new-session and resume flows, plus parser coverage for the no-fast OpenRouter case.
4. [Completed] Run targeted regressions and the full validation gates on the final tree.

## Validation Evidence

- Targeted regressions passed: `cargo test codex_model_suffix_parses_reasoning_effort_and_fast_mode -- --nocapture`, `cargo test openrouter_new_session_fast_suffix_model_is_passed_through_unchanged -- --nocapture`, and `cargo test openrouter_resume_fast_suffix_model_is_passed_through_unchanged -- --nocapture`.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed after formatting the updated files.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix build` passed and produced `/nix/store/1mkxbh06ly3p2jzq2rlb1xgsnj980cy9-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0`.

## Outstanding Risks

None identified.
